name: Oritone scientific colormaps
description: Scientific visualization colormaps for Oritone. The color direction references the Okabe & Ito color-blind-friendly palette.

generation.samples: 256 for sequential maps; 257 for diverging maps to include an exact midpoint sample
generation.interpolation: custom sampled ramps generated in OKLab with controlled lightness; exported RGB points use ParaView Lab interpolation metadata
generation.notes: Sequential maps are monotonic in lightness. Diverging maps use mirrored lightness around the center.

maps.name: Oritone Delta
maps.description: Symmetric blue-to-warm diverging map with a high-lightness neutral center. Designed for signed anomalies, residuals, and centered scalar fields.
maps.intended_use: Diverging / symmetric: use for data with a meaningful zero or midpoint; set the scalar range symmetrically around that point.

maps.name: Oritone Balance
maps.description: Symmetric teal-to-rose diverging map with equalized endpoint lightness and a warm neutral center. Useful when blue/orange semantics would conflict with the data story.
maps.intended_use: Diverging / symmetric: signed pressure, displacement, uncertainty difference, or centered normalized quantities.

maps.name: Oritone Ember
maps.description: Asymmetric warm sequential map using one dominant amber/ember hue family with monotonic lightness. High contrast without rainbow-like hue jumps.
maps.intended_use: Sequential / asymmetric: scalar intensity, energy, heat, flux, or positive-only quantities.

maps.name: Oritone Marine
maps.description: Asymmetric blue-cyan sequential map with monotonic lightness and strong low-to-high contrast. Tuned for continuous fields where a cool scalar scale reads naturally.
maps.intended_use: Sequential / asymmetric: depth, density, concentration, velocity magnitude, or fields conventionally shown with cool colors.

maps.name: Oritone Canopy
maps.description: Asymmetric blue-green-to-amber sequential map with three dominant color regions and monotonic lightness. Emphasizes mid-to-high scalar structure while preserving order.
maps.intended_use: Sequential / asymmetric: ecology, terrain overlays, scalar concentration, material fraction, and positive-valued simulation results.

maps.name: Oritone Fusion
maps.description: Asymmetric multi-hue sequential map moving from muted purple through blue and green into amber. Built for broad dynamic range while keeping a monotonic lightness ramp.
maps.intended_use: Sequential / asymmetric: general-purpose quantitative scalar fields with wide dynamic range; use when a single-hue scale is too subtle.
